meta.note: March 16 evening run — YouTube as 'Creators are new studios' (INMA), publishers hiring YouTube-specialist producers (DCN), EditShare Analytical AI at NAB 2026, LucidLink Classic upgrade tool, TikTok Bulletin Boards for publishers

decisionPanels.title: 🚨 Iran Conflict Day 3 — Guardian sustained output, MMR endurance test
decisionPanels.summary: Guardian is now into day 3 of the US-Iran conflict. Today's output: 'Trump wages war on Iran his own way: commander-in-chaos' (David Smith analysis, published ~3pm London). This is a multi-day, high-volume multimedia event — the kind that will define whether MMR's architecture is production-grade. The key risk has shifted from speed (can we publish fast?) to endurance (can we maintain quality vertical output over 72+ hours without team burnout or workflow degradation?). Any friction in LucidLink ingest, iconik metadata, or Wildmoka distribution is magnified under sustained conflict load. This is live data for the MMR handover docs.
actions: CAPTURE: Ask multimedia producers today or tomorrow — what broke, what was slow, what worked? This debrief data is more valuable than any vendor demo.
DOCUMENT: Log which workflow steps became bottlenecks under sustained conflict load. This becomes the opening section of the MMR risk register.
MONITOR: Guardian continues to be at maximum editorial intensity. Don't push new tooling changes during the hot phase — wait for the acute period to pass.

decisionPanels.title: 📺 YouTube: 'Creators are the New Studios' — Guardian's competitive set just changed
decisionPanels.summary: INMA analysis of YouTube CEO Neal Mohan's 2026 letter (published 3 weeks ago, now being acted on) makes the competitive reality clear: Guardian is no longer competing mainly against other publishers for YouTube audience. It is competing against self-funded creator businesses that move faster, cost less, and own their audience data directly. YouTube is also not picking a format — Shorts (200bn daily views), long-form, podcasts, live, shopping all coexist in one product. The key phrase: 'Platforms are native to format shifts. Publishers are retrofitting them.' Publishers hiring YouTube-specialist video producers confirms this is now operational strategy, not theory (DCN, March 12). YouTube is UK's #2 streaming service (behind BBC, ahead of ITV). That is the audience Guardian's YouTube strategy is competing for.
actions: HIRE (or advocate): The 'YouTube-specialist video producer' role is now industry-standard. Guardian Studios should have one if it doesn't. This person is distinct from a general video producer — they understand the platform's algorithmic logic.
RETHINK: MMR's Project Launcher needs a YouTube-native output type — not just 'upload here' but structured workflow for Shorts + long-form + thumbnail + metadata optimised for YouTube discovery.

TEST: YouTube live control room vertical layout (March 13 feature) on Guardian's next live event — still free, zero engineering. This is the fastest route to testing the vertical format with real audience data.

decisionPanels.title: 🤖 Analytical AI vs Generative AI — the MAM distinction that matters at NAB 2026
decisionPanels.summary: EditShare's NAB 2026 preview (March 11) has crystallised an important distinction for Guardian's AI governance: Analytical AI (speech recognition, face detection, scene tagging, text analysis — what EditShare FLOW AI and iconik's AI do) vs Generative AI (creating new content — what ChatGPT/Sora/Veo do). Analytical AI is the workhorse of the MAM world: it auto-tags, makes archives searchable, routes assets, and removes manual data entry. Backlight already uses it (11.4M AI uses in their 2026 stats report). INMA's YouTube analysis adds another layer: YouTube CEO describes AI as 'a substrate' — not a feature, but the operating layer everything sits on. For Guardian: the AI governance document for MMR should explicitly separate Analytical AI (safe, already in iconik) from Generative AI (requires editorial guardrails). This framing will help the SPUR coalition conversation too.
actions: DRAFT: Add an AI governance section to MMR documentation that distinguishes Analytical AI (metadata, search, tagging — already in iconik) from Generative AI (content creation — requires editorial policy).
BRIEF: Use EditShare/FLOW AI as a reference point when explaining iconik's AI features to producers. 'It finds and tags — it doesn't write or generate.' Reduces anxiety around AI use in the workflow.
NAB WATCH: EditShare at N1251 and Backlight at N2829 — both showcasing Analytical AI at NAB (April 18-22). Compare approaches when announcements land.

decisionPanels.title: 🏭 NAB 2026 (April 18-22): 33 days — Harmonic, Avid, Backlight, EditShare all confirmed
decisionPanels.summary: The vendor picture for NAB 2026 is now clearer than ever. Confirmed exhibitors directly relevant to Guardian MMR: Backlight/iconik (N2829), Harmonic Spectrum X Plus (W2831), Avid AI workflows, EditShare FLOW AI + MediaSilo AI (N1251). Four of Guardian's most relevant media tech vendors in one room over five days. EditShare's MediaSilo AI announcement (new bundles: Teams/Pro/Enterprise, unlimited AI, forensic watermarking, SSO) is also a direct signal about where the MAM+review market is heading — all-in-one AI intelligence, not bolt-on. This is the context in which Backlight will make any iconik announcements. Expect AI metadata, archive activation, and multi-format publishing to dominate all four vendor stories.
actions: PLAN: Decide NAB 2026 attendance before April 1. Even a two-day trip covers all four vendor booths. Highest-value media tech event of the year for MMR context.

PREP: Build a Backlight/iconik question list focused on: (1) Analytical AI auto-tagging improvements, (2) liquid content metadata fields, (3) audio workflow enhancements, (4) any LucidLink/Frame.io integration updates.
WATCH: EditShare MediaSilo AI new bundles (Teams/Pro/Enterprise) — if pricing is competitive, worth evaluating as an external review platform for Guardian's post-production partners.

items.title: iconik changelog March 5, 2026: My Shares filtering + favourite saved searches — confirmed no new changelog items March 6-16

LucidLink Upgrade Tool (March 2, 2026): upgrade Classic Filespaces to new LucidLink in-place — no data copying or migration required; Guardian WIP storage upgrade path now confirmed
LucidLink Connect GA: mount S3 buckets as LucidLink Filespaces without data migration — byte-range streaming, SOC 2 Type II + MPA TPN certified
Wildmoka supports publishing to social drafts (Facebook/Instagram drafts, YouTube unlisted) — batch social scheduling workflow available now for Guardian social teams without live-publish risk

Guardian Iran Day 3 (March 16): 'Trump wages war on Iran his own way: commander-in-chaos' — Day 3 of sustained conflict publishing; MMR under endurance test
Guardian SPUR coalition: BBC, FT, Sky News, Telegraph building AI content standards — UK AI copyright delay strengthens early-mover position; MMR schema should incorporate rights/licensing fields
Guardian Studios: Leah Green (ex-Channel 4) as head — personality-led journalism, video-first mandate; Project Launcher must support journalist-as-creator social publishing workflows
Guardian US 30M audience (+21% YoY, Nov 2025 Comscore): highest US traffic of any UK newspaper brand — US audience is the growth engine; YouTube is the distribution layer

INMA March 2026: YouTube CEO 'Creators are the new studios' — publishers competing against self-funded creators with lower costs + better audience data; YouTube is UK's #2 streaming service (behind BBC, ahead of ITV)
DCN March 12: publishers now actively hiring YouTube-specialist video producers — YouTube climbing priority lists in 2026 as AI cuts search referral traffic by up to 79%
Reuters Institute 2026: YouTube main publisher focus (+74 net score); TikTok +56, Instagram +41; publishers deprioritising Facebook (-23) and X (-52); search traffic -40% over 3 years
TikTok Bulletin Boards rolling out to publishers/brands in For You feed (March 13) — direct audience notification tool; Guardian should test alongside YouTube live control room vertical
YouTube March 13: live stream → instant vertical layout in live control room — zero-cost Shorts from any live feed, zero engineering required
AWS Elemental Inference: AI real-time vertical video (9:16) from live feeds at 6-10s latency — Fox Sports + NBCUniversal live; live breaking news vertical publishing now viable

EditShare FLOW AI + MediaSilo AI at NAB 2026 (N1251): Analytical AI for auto-metadata (speech, faces, scenes, text) — transforms archives into searchable assets without manual tagging; new MediaSilo bundles with unlimited AI

INMA: YouTube CEO says AI is now 'a substrate not a feature' — 20M users using Ask feature; AI Shorts generation; autodubbing at scale. Publishers treating AI as assistive tool risk falling behind audience expectations
Reuters Institute 2026: 'liquid content' + AEO (Answer Engine Optimization) + search -40% — MMR iconik metadata schema must support format-agnostic liquid content packaging from creation
Harmonic Spectrum X Plus for NAB 2026: next-gen media server — simplified ingest/playout, lower cost per channel, new AI-driven workflow innovations (W2831)
Agentic AI for newsrooms: 2026 is the year real-world impact arrives — multi-step autonomous agents now in metadata, compliance, rights, scheduling at well-resourced newsrooms
YouTube pre-publish check tool expanding — detects more potential policy violations before upload; reduces risk of Guardian content being flagged post-publish on conflict/breaking news coverage

changes.summary: March 16 run: Guardian enters Day 3 of Iran conflict publishing. Two high-signal new sources tonight: INMA's analysis of YouTube CEO's 2026 letter ('Creators are the new studios') with direct publisher implications, and Digital Content Next confirming publishers are now operationalising YouTube strategies by hiring specialist producers. EditShare's NAB 2026 preview adds important context for the Analytical AI vs Generative AI distinction — a framing that will be useful for Guardian's MMR AI governance documentation. New LucidLink Upgrade Tool (March 2) added — confirms Guardian's WIP filespace can be upgraded in-place without data migration. TikTok Bulletin Boards now rolling to publishers in For You feed — new audience notification tool to evaluate. No new iconik changelog since March 5. No new LucidLink product release since January/March 5 Connect GA. NAB 2026 now 33 days away.
